title: Furniture makers and other craft woodworkers
description: Create or reproduce handmade objects for sale and exhibition using a variety of techniques, such as welding, weaving, pottery, and needlecraft. Construct, erect, install, or repair structures and fixtures made of wood and comparable materials, such as concrete forms; building frameworks, including partitions, joists, studding, and rafters; and wood stairways, window and door frames, and hardwood floors. May also install cabinets, siding, drywall, and batt or roll insulation. Includes brattice builders who build doors or brattices (ventilation walls or partitions) in underground passageways. Assemble, install, or maintain solar photovoltaic (PV) systems on roofs or other structures in compliance with site assessment and schematics. May include measuring, cutting, assembling, and bolting structural framing and solar modules. May perform minor electrical work such as current checks. (Based on 21 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Trim wood parts according to specifications using planes, chisels, wood files, or sanders.
Grease or oil woodworking machines and clean or maintain products, machines, or work areas.
Control hoists and operate hydraulic lifts to remove parts or products from workstations and shape wood into desired forms.
Unclamp workpieces and remove them from machines after processing.
Operate gluing machines to glue pieces of wood together and apply glue to wood surfaces.
Set up, program, or control computer-aided design (CAD) or computer numerical control (CNC) machines and tend manual woodworking machines.
Examine raw woodstock for defects and conformity to size and specification standards before processing.
Attach and adjust guides, stops, clamps, chucks, or feed mechanisms using hand tools.
Secure woodstock in holding devices, on conveyors, or in hoppers for machine processing.
Remove and replace worn parts, bits, and tools on machines to ensure optimal operation.
Inspect and adjust safety devices and machine components to ensure safe operation during woodworking processes.
Change alignment of machine guides to prevent defects in finished products and conduct trial cuts to verify machine settings.
Feed stock through machines such as planers and sanders to produce the desired components.
Measure and mark cutting lines on materials using appropriate tools for accuracy.
Shape or cut materials to specified measurements using various hand tools and machinery.
Install and adjust fixtures such as windows, frames, and trims in various structures.
Study specifications in blueprints or sketches to prepare project layout and determine materials required.
Inspect structures for damage and perform repairs or replacements as needed.
Install insulation materials in building structures to improve energy efficiency.
Repair or replace structural components in buildings, including cabinets and other fixtures, using woodworking techniques.
Maintain accurate job records and prepare cost estimates for projects based on specifications.
Comply with prescribed safety procedures or national laws regulating waste disposal methods.
Draw up detailed specifications and discuss projects with customers.
Fill and smooth cracks or depressions, and remove imperfections, using putty, glue, nails, or screws.
Fit, fasten, and assemble wood parts together to form patterns, models, or sections, using glue, nails, dowels, and screws.
Brush, spray, or hand-rub finishing ingredients onto wood surfaces, applying lacquer or other sealers.
Smooth, shape, and touch up surfaces to prepare them for finishing, using sandpaper, chisels, or grinders.
Select appropriate finishing ingredients such as paint, stain, lacquer, or varnish based on wood type.
Examine furniture to determine the extent of damage, deciding on the best method for repair or restoration.
Construct wooden models, patterns, templates, and molds for products.
Set up, operate, and adjust woodworking machines to cut and shape sections, parts, and patterns.
Remove old finishes and damaged parts, using tools like sandpaper or solvents.
Install hardware such as hinges, handles, and pulls using hand tools.
Repair or alter wooden furniture, cabinetry, and fixtures.
Apply coatings, using spray guns or brushes to protect or decorate surfaces.
Clean and maintain tools and equipment using appropriate cleaning materials.
Measure and mark dimensions of parts on lumber stock prior to cutting, following blueprints for accuracy.
Verify dimensions and check the quality of pieces to ensure adherence to specifications.
Monitor machine operations to detect malfunctions and resolve issues.
Prepare workpieces for etching or engraving by cutting, sanding, cleaning, or polishing them.
Inspect etched work for depth, uniformity, and defects, using calibrated instruments.
Engrave and print patterns, designs, or lettering onto various surfaces using hand tools or power tools.
Sketch or draw objects to be crafted and develop concepts for craft objects.
Select materials for use based on characteristics such as strength, color, and texture.
Plan and attend craft shows to market products and use media for advertising.
Create prototypes or models of objects to be crafted using various methods.
Cut, shape, fit, join, or otherwise process materials using hand tools, power tools, or machinery.
Requisition materials, supplies, tools, or equipment parts needed for production.
Conduct or arrange training for employees on equipment operations, safety procedures, or maintenance techniques.
